layman_headline: Crypto market stuck in fear mode — no clear bargain worth jumping into yet.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dropping 2% and most altcoins getting hit even harder. Bitcoin dominance at 56% means money is flowing away from smaller coins and into Bitcoin, which is a tough environment for altcoin swing trades. While a few coins like JTO and ETHFI show small signs of life, none of them have a compelling enough setup with strong enough signals to justify risking money right now. The smart move is to stay patient and wait for a cleaner entry.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch from the sidelines. The market is fearful but hasn't reached the extreme levels that historically produce the best buying opportunities. Wait for either a sharper selloff (Fear & Greed below 15) or a clear reversal pattern before committing funds.
expert_reasoning: Across all 9 swing candidates, no coin presents a high-conviction setup. JTO has the best chart structure (slightly bullish, above 2 MAs) but RSI at 59 signals it's already recovering and may be extended after today's +7.5% bounce. ETHFI has a bullish Marubozu candle but resistance is only 2.5% away giving a terrible 0.11x risk/reward. GRASS dropped 22.5% in a week which screams mean-reversion, but the bearish engulfing candle and -0.9 chart bias score warn that selling pressure is accelerating, not exhausting. BTC dominance at 56% is a strong headwind for all alt positions. The Fear & Greed index at 28 is improving from 24.6 weekly average, but hasn't reached the extreme fear levels (sub-15) where contrarian buys have historically produced the best results per our signal credibility data (80% win rate on Fear_Greed signals).
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56% continues rising, draining liquidity from all altcoins regardless of individual merit
Fear & Greed at 28 is fearful but not extreme — further downside is plausible before a genuine bottom
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate — the reliable 100% win-rate signal is absent
watch_list: Bitcoin holding $60,000 support — a break below could trigger another wave of alt selling
Fear & Greed index trend — if it drops below 20 that would set up higher conviction contrarian entries
JTO price action at $0.60 support — if it holds and RSI drops to 40-45, that becomes a much stronger entry
market_context: Crypto markets are in a risk-off phase with BTC at $62.7K, Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear), and high BTC dominance squeezing altcoins. Bitcoin ETF inflows snapped an 8-week outflow streak which is mildly encouraging, but geopolitical uncertainty from U.S.-Iran tensions adds downside risk.
context_banner: Market fear is elevated but not extreme — patience pays more than forcing a trade in this environment.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) is improving from weekly average of 24.6, but not yet at extreme levels where our 80% win-rate contrarian signal triggers most reliably.
Bitcoin ETFs drew $197M snapping an 8-week outflow streak — a structural positive but not yet reflected in price action, keeping conviction low.
No candidate is in oversold territory (sub-30 RSI); JTO at 59 is the most active but approaching overbought territory after a bounce.
No volume breakout signals detected across any candidate — our highest-confidence signal (100% win rate, +13.4% avg return) is completely absent.
Bitcoin nearing a Fidelity-tracked power law support line from 2015 suggests BTC may find a floor soon, but geopolitical risk from Iran strikes adds uncertainty.
